_meta.description: Original open-ended agronomy questions with reference answers and required-substring keys. Authored in-repo — see THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md.

cases.question: What does the re-entry interval (REI) on a pesticide label mean?
cases.reference: The REI is the re-entry interval: the minimum time that must pass after a pesticide application before workers may safely re-enter the treated area without protective equipment.

cases.question: What does the pre-harvest interval (PHI) tell a grower?
cases.reference: The PHI is the pre-harvest interval: the minimum number of days that must elapse between the last pesticide application and harvest, to keep residues within legal limits.

cases.question: Give one benefit of planting a winter cover crop.
cases.reference: Cover crops reduce soil erosion, suppress weeds, add organic matter, and can fix nitrogen, improving soil health between cash crops.

cases.question: Why do farmers rotate crops across seasons?
cases.reference: Crop rotation breaks pest and disease cycles, manages nutrient demand, and reduces reliance on a single input, improving long-term yields.

cases.question: Why should a grower take a soil test before applying fertiliser?
cases.reference: A soil test reveals nutrient levels and pH so fertiliser is applied only where needed, avoiding waste, runoff, and over-application.

cases.question: What role does scouting play in integrated pest management?
cases.reference: Scouting monitors pest populations against action thresholds so controls are applied only when justified, rather than on a fixed calendar.

cases.question: How does mulching help a vegetable bed?
cases.reference: Mulch conserves soil moisture, suppresses weeds, moderates soil temperature, and reduces erosion.

cases.question: Why is good field drainage important for most crops?
cases.reference: Good drainage prevents waterlogging, which starves roots of oxygen and promotes root diseases, so it protects yield and root health.
